metaTitle: PDF Merger — Combine Multiple PDF Files (Free, Private & Offline)
metaDescription: Merge multiple PDF documents into a single file instantly in your browser. 100% private — your files never leave your device. No limits on file size or count.

howto.description: Drag and drop multiple PDF files or click to upload. Your documents are processed locally and never sent to a server.
Drag the file thumbnails to reorder them. The final PDF will follow the exact sequence you set here.
Check the file names and page counts to ensure everything is correct before clicking the 'Merge' button.
Click 'Merge PDF' to generate the new file. It will be available for instant download to your device.

why: 100% private — your sensitive documents never leave your browser
Combine unlimited PDF files into a single document
Intuitive drag-and-drop interface for easy reordering
Perfect for combining reports, receipts, contracts, or portfolio pieces
Original quality preserved — no re-compression during merging
Works completely offline after the initial page load
No registration, no file size limits, completely free forever

faq.q: Is my sensitive data safe when merging PDFs?
faq.a: Yes. This tool uses client-side processing, meaning the merging logic happens inside your browser's memory. No data is ever uploaded to our servers, making it ideal for legal or financial documents.

faq.q: How many PDFs can I merge at once?
faq.a: There is no hard limit on the number of files. However, merging a very large number of high-resolution PDFs (e.g., 50+ files) may depend on your device's available memory (RAM).

faq.q: Will the merged PDF lose quality?
faq.a: No. The tool combines the existing data streams of your PDFs without re-encoding them. The text sharpness and image quality will remain identical to the original files.
No. For security reasons, you must unlock any encrypted PDFs before merging them. This ensures the browser can read and join the files locally without needing access to your passwords.

faq.q: Does the order of files matter?
faq.a: Yes. The order in which you arrange the files on the screen is the exact order in which they will appear in the final combined PDF document.

faq.q: Can I use this on my mobile phone?
faq.a: Yes! This tool is fully responsive and works on any modern web browser, whether you are on a desktop, tablet, or smartphone.
